operation: First, a rectangular box with dimensions 15 length, 15 width, and 3 height is created from an XY-plane workplane. Next, the top positive-Z face of the box is selected to define a new workplane, and the origin of this workplane is offset 7.5 units along the X axis and 7.5 units along the Y axis to align with one corner of the top face. Finally, a through countersunk hole is cut at this offset origin: it has a 5 unit diameter cylindrical through bore, an 8 unit diameter countersink opening, an 82 degree countersink angle, and passes fully through the 3 unit thick plate.
geometry: This model is a thin square prism of uniform thickness, with a single countersunk through hole placed at one of its four corners. The hole has a conical countersunk recess opening at the top surface of the prism, which tapers down to a straight cylindrical bore that runs completely through the plate to its bottom surface.
function: This is a compact square mounting spacer or bracket designed to be secured with a flat-head fastener via its corner countersunk hole, which ensures the fastener head sits flush with the plate's top surface to avoid protrusion during assembly, suitable for use as a simple mounting adapter, shim, or positioning component in mechanical assemblies.
